 {"id":"franken_networkx-opl9","title":"[D1] Strict/Hardened mode decision: implement (recommended) vs. retract","description":"**Track:** D — Modes. **Priority:** P0. **Type:** task.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-09T00:23:26.167651233Z","updated_at":"2026-04-15T04:15:40.118259692Z","closed_at":"2026-04-15T04:15:40.115386096Z","close_reason":"Documented the explicit decision to keep and complete strict/hardened mode instead of retracting it; README, FEATURE_PARITY, and the bridge plan now treat D2-D4 as implementation/proof follow-ons.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-owcd","title":"NX-REPLACE Quick-wins: rewire 6 functions that already have Rust impls","description":"## Functions (6) — already implemented in Rust, just need Python rewiring
These functions currently delegate to NX via _to_nx() even though Rust implementations exist:

1. minimum_edge_cut (__init__.py:1360) — Rust has minimum_st_edge_cut_edmonds_karp
2. minimum_st_node_cut (__init__.py:3328) — Rust has minimum_node_cut / minimum_node_cut_directed
3. ego_graph (__init__.py:1383) — Rust has ego_graph, binding exists at algorithms.rs:9262
4. create_empty_copy (__init__.py:1211) — trivial: Graph() + add_nodes_from(G.nodes(data=True))
5. node_degree_xy (__init__.py:6028) — Rust has node_degree_xy, binding at algorithms.rs:9480
6. all_pairs_node_connectivity (__init__.py:3305) — can loop over existing node_connectivity

## Why highest priority
Zero Rust work needed — just change the Python wrapper to call the existing Rust binding instead of converting to NX. Each is a 5-minute fix.

## Implementation plan
For each function:
1. Remove 'import networkx as nx' and '_to_nx()' call
2. Replace with direct call to _fnx.function_name_rust(G, ...) or use existing Python-level FNX API
3. Handle parameter differences (e.g., minimum_edge_cut needs s,t params)

## Testing requirements
Add to tests/python/test_quickwin_rewire_parity.py:
- test_minimum_edge_cut_matches_nx: compare cut value and cut set size
- test_minimum_st_node_cut_matches_nx: compare for specific s,t pairs
- test_ego_graph_matches_nx: verify nodes/edges match for radius=1,2
- test_create_empty_copy: verify same nodes, zero edges, attrs preserved
- test_node_degree_xy_matches_nx: compare (in_deg, out_deg) pairs
- test_all_pairs_node_connectivity_matches_nx: compare full dict

Run: pytest tests/python/test_quickwin_rewire_parity.py -v --tb=long

## Files to modify
- python/franken_networkx/__init__.py: rewire 6 functions","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-03T02:43:24.254573322Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-03T13:55:41.487938172Z","closed_at":"2026-04-03T13:55:41.487651209Z","close_reason":"Already complete — minimum_edge_cut, minimum_st_node_cut, create_empty_copy, node_degree_xy, all_pairs_node_connectivity all already have Rust fast paths. ego_graph has Rust path for common case (NX fallback only for distance/undirected params, which is acceptable conditional delegation).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"franken_networkx-owq","title":"PERF: greedy_modularity_communities is 2000x slower than NetworkX","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-02T20:55:13.967369735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-02T21:00:17.320372835Z","closed_at":"2026-04-02T21:00:17.320115879Z","close_reason":"Rewrote from O(n^4) naive pairwise scan to heap-based CNM algorithm with sparse delta-Q matrix. Performance went from 540s to 0.1s on 1000-node graph (5400x improvement, now 2.7x faster than NetworkX). Correctness verified on barbell, K5-barbell, and path graphs.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
